summary: `if`, `elif`, and `else` control code execution paths. Conditions evaluate top-down; the first `True` branch executes and remaining branches are skipped.
oneLineSummary: Conditional branching executes the first true block in a sequence of checks.
keyTakeaways: Order conditions from most specific to least specific.

memoryTricks.trick: Think of a train track switch — once the switch is set to one track, the train travels down that path only.

commonErrors.fix: Ensure all statements in block share identical indent level

nextTopics.title: Nested Conditions & Ternary Operators
nextTopics.whyNext: Learn inline conditionals and nested decision trees.
